title: Cicer incisum
intro: Cicer incisum is a perennial legume plant native to the Western Himalayas, commonly known as the "cut-leaved chickpea" or "Indian chickpea." The seeds of Cicer incisum have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cicer incisum seeds include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cicer incisum is often prepared as a powder or decoction, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulations to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cicer incisum is classified as a species of the genus Cicer, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. This classification places it within the legume family, which consists of plants that have fruit in the form of pods. Cicer incisum is a wild chickpea species and is closely related to domesticated chickpeas. The genus Cicer also includes other wild chickpea species, which are native to the Middle East and South Asia. The classification of Cicer incisum is a key aspect of its taxonomic identity and evolutionary history.
